response.justification: Maria Rodriguez is a well-rounded engineer with genuine geospatial exposure and proven distributed-systems work, but she does not meet the core technical requirement of the role. The position demands modern C++ or Rust expertise for building high-performance routing data pipelines; her C++ exposure is limited to a single role in 2014–2015 (NI LabWindows components) and a student thesis project, and Rust is entirely absent from her profile. Her geospatial work through GISCollective and Green Map is web-based (Node.js, Ember.js), not the systems-level C++ pipeline engineering required for routing tile generation. The language mismatch is too fundamental for a mid-senior IC role where C++ fluency is the primary technical prerequisite.
strengths: Genuine geospatial domain experience through GISCollective and Green Map, demonstrating familiarity with GIS concepts, event-driven data platforms, and real-world deployment to conservation and mapping communities

Proven high-throughput, low-latency systems work at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O, fibers, and high-performance caching — transferable distributed-systems intuition
Strong Linux and infrastructure depth across multiple roles including Kubernetes, Docker, CI/CD, and DevOps tooling, which aligns with the Linux server application environment of the role
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and over a decade old — one role in 2014–2015 writing components for NI LabWindows and a bachelor thesis project; Rust is not mentioned anywhere, leaving the primary language requirement entirely unmet

All geospatial work is web-stack based (Node.js, Ember.js, REST APIs) with no evidence of C++ geospatial data processing, routing graph construction, tile generation, or navigation algorithm implementation

Current and recent career trajectory (TypeScript, Node.js, AI agent tooling, MCP frameworks) is moving further from systems-level C++ engineering, indicating the gap would require substantial retraining rather than a manageable ramp-up

key_factors.factor: No routing or navigation pipeline experience
